compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Why
Agentic steps need a generalized output contract, a safe repo view at a caller-chosen git ref,
scoped ticket access, and per-step model choice.

## What
- Generalize RunRequest with optional output_schema/mode; split findings-finalization into one
  strategy (backward-compatible with existing review_* callers).
- Hardened git-ref filesystem: rev-parse to a full SHA, `git archive` that SHA, hardened tar
  extract (read-only/no-git steps); handle export-ignore/subst/LFS/submodule/symlink; size
  guard; finally + TTL GC.
- Scoped rebar ticket tool (read + comment only). Multi-provider/model per step.
- Agent runtime hardening: create_agent v1, exact dependency pins, robust structured output.

## Scope
- In: runner generalization, git-ref snapshot (create + teardown + split trigger), scoped tool,
  per-step provider, agent runtime hardening.
- Out: gate security / provenance hardening (epic 8a1c).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] RunRequest output_schema/mode added; review_ticket/review_code/scan_spec unaffected.
- [ ] git archive snapshot bound to the resolved SHA; hardened extraction; documented export-ignore/submodule behavior.
- [ ] WS-D owns snapshot creation + finally-teardown and the fs_tools.py -> llm/workflow/snapshot.py split trigger.
- [ ] Scoped ticket tool grants read + comment only (no write/transition/sign).
- [ ] Per-step model/provider precedence documented (step > workflow > config > env > default).
- [ ] Built on create_agent v1 with exact-pinned langgraph/-prebuilt/-checkpoint (upgrades gated behind WS-G evals).
- [ ] Structured output: Pydantic v2 (all fields required), include_raw=True, branch on parsing_error/parsed-is-None with own retry+repair, non-empty invalid_tool_calls treated as retryable.
- [ ] Preserves AIMessage(tool_calls)<->ToolMessage pairing across WORKFLOW_STEP replay.
compiled_state.title: WS-D: Agentic-step capabilities (runner generalization, git-ref snapshot, multi-provider)
